title: Text Inspection, Search, and Structured Transformation · Command Brief: Find Selection Action
summary: Extract a defensible incident summary from mixed logs without silently corrupting delimiters, encodings, ordering, or duplicate policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux find selection action increment in a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate find traversal, predicates, grouping, pruning, printing, and actions while protecting path boundaries.
Use fixed strings, basic or extended regular expressions, anchors, classes, context, and exit status with explicit locale assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: linux-find-selection-action — Separate find traversal, predicates, grouping, pruning, printing, and actions while protecting path boundaries.
LC_ALL=C awk -F, 'NR > 1 { print $2, $1 }' data/incidents.csv \
  | sort -k1,1 -k2,2 \
  | uniq -c
